error_signature: file not found for script execution
title: File not found for executing script
symptom: The script fails to execute because it cannot locate the downloaded PDF file from Google Drive.
applies_when: Using Google Drive node to download files
Using Execute Command node to run a script
not_this_if: The PDF file is already saved to a known location
The script does not require a file input
root_cause: When files are downloaded using the Google Drive node, they are stored in memory as binary data and not saved to a physical location. The Execute Command node requires a file path to execute scripts, leading to errors if the file is not accessible.
fix_steps: Add a 'Write Binary File' node to your workflow to save the binary data to a temporary file.
Set the 'Binary Property' to 'data' and specify a file path like '/tmp/downloaded_file.pdf' or 'C:\Temp\downloaded_file.pdf'.
Connect the 'Write Binary File' node to the 'Execute Command' node to ensure the script can access the temporary file.
agent_instruction: Use the 'Write Binary File' node to save the downloaded PDF to a temporary location before executing your script.
